title: $fieldendmod
text: $fieldendmod Return the modification status of a field when the field is exited. $fieldendmod { ( Field ) } Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description Field String Field name. It can optionally contain a qualified field name, for example MYFLD.MYENT . If omitted, the current field is assumed. Return Values Table 2. Value Meaning 0 Field has not been modified. 1 Field has been modified or if $fieldendmod has been set for the current field. Use Use in the loseFocus trigger of Form components. Description The $fieldendmod indicates whether the user has modified data in the field. It is set to 0 in the following circumstances: When the user enters the field. By resetting the $fieldcheck function. When data of a field is modified in ProcScript. Each time a component is restarted (unless Component Behavior is set to Keep component definition and data in memory ). By the following ProcScript statements clear erase release reload retrieve store In contrast, the function $fieldmod indicates whether data has been modified during its lifetime in the component. For example, if the user modifies a field and leaves it, $fieldendmod is 1 (in the loseFocus trigger); $fieldmod is also 1. If the user then returns to the field and leaves it again without changing it, $fieldendmod is 0, while $fieldmod remains 1. Example: Using $fieldendmod The following example checks the value of $fieldendmod , then checks to make sure that the value of the field is less than 120: trigger loseFocus ; (of field AGE) if ($fieldendmod = 1) if (AGE >= 120) message "That is far too old!" return -1 endif endif edn; loseFocus Copy Related Links $fieldcheck Return or set the requirement for field validation. $occmod Return the modification status of an occurrence. trigger loseFocus (Field) Trigger that defines what happens after a user successfully leaves a field in a form component.
